questions.stem: What is the primary mechanism of action of vancomycin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: inhibits the 30S ribosomal subunit to block protein synthesis

choices.label: B
choices.text: inhibits the transpeptidase that cross-links peptidoglycan in the cell wall

choices.label: C
choices.text: blocks folic acid synthesis by acting as an antimetabolite

choices.label: D
choices.text: blocks cell wall synthesis by binding peptidoglycan precursors

questions.explanation: vancomycin blocks cell wall synthesis by binding peptidoglycan precursors.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

hints.prompt: Antibiotics achieve selective toxicity by attacking a structure that bacteria have but human cells lack. Which structure is a classic bacteria-only drug target?
hints.rationale: Only bacteria build a peptidoglycan cell wall, so it is a common selectively toxic drug target.

hints.prompt: Vancomycin is a bulky molecule that cannot easily cross the membrane and acts outside the cell. Which target process does that point to?
hints.rationale: A drug that acts outside the membrane targets cell-wall synthesis, ruling out intracellular ribosome, folate, or DNA targets.

hints.prompt: One choice says the drug 'inhibits the transpeptidase that cross-links peptidoglycan.' How should you treat that choice?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a trap: it names a real cell-wall enzyme step, but that is only one possible wall mechanism, not automatically the right one

choices.label: B
choices.text: As obviously correct simply because it mentions peptidoglycan

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a description of blocking the 30S ribosome

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a description of blocking folate synthesis

hints.rationale: Inhibiting the cross-linking enzyme is a distinct cell-wall mechanism, so this tempting choice should not be assumed correct.

choices.text: the innate-receptor (Toll-like receptor) and whole-response (polyclonal) options

hints.prompt: A tempting distractor is 'a monoclonal antibody.' What is that?

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: the joined structure of two bound molecules

hints.rationale: A monoclonal antibody is a single antibody type, not a bound pair, so it is a trap.

hints.prompt: Self-reactive immune cells are dangerous. Mechanisms that stop them from attacking the body are collectively called what?
hints.rationale: Preventing attack on self is achieved by mechanisms of self-tolerance.

hints.prompt: The stem specifies self-reactive cells that have already REACHED THE TISSUES. This points to tolerance occurring where?

choices.text: outside the primary lymphoid organs, in the tissues (the periphery)

choices.label: B
choices.text: only in the bone marrow before release

choices.label: C
choices.text: only in the thymus during development

choices.label: D
choices.text: only in the bloodstream at birth

hints.rationale: 'Reach the tissues' indicates peripheral tolerance, not central tolerance in marrow or thymus.

hints.prompt: A tempting choice is 'clonal deletion.' Which statement about clonal deletion is correct, so you can decide whether it fits?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It removes self-reactive cells centrally during their development, before they reach the tissues

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is the antibody class-switch process

choices.label: C
choices.text: It increases antibody affinity over time

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is a type of granulocyte

hints.rationale: Clonal deletion acts centrally during development, which does not match self-reactive cells already in the tissues.

hints.prompt: The immune system recognizes a virus mainly by its surface proteins. In general, why does a change in these surface proteins matter to us?

questions.stem: In metabolism, what is the number of pyruvate molecules produced from one glucose in glycolysis?

hints.prompt: Glycolysis makes pyruvate, ATP, and NADH from glucose. The question asks only for the number of PYRUVATE per glucose. Answering it is mainly a matter of what?

choices.text: carbon accounting (how glucose's six carbons are divided)

hints.prompt: Two options are clearly NOT a count of pyruvate: one is an electron-carrier count and one is zero (yet glycolysis does yield pyruvate). Which two can be set aside?

hints.prompt: A tempting distractor is 'about 30 to 32.' What does that number represent?

hints.rationale: 30 to 32 is the total ATP yield of aerobic respiration, a classic trap for the pyruvate count.

questions.stem: Which feature is characteristic of mitochondrial inheritance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: both alleles are fully and separately expressed in the heterozygote, as in AB blood type

choices.label: B
choices.text: an affected individual usually has at least one affected parent, and both sexes are affected equally

choices.label: C
choices.text: the trait passes from an affected mother to all of her children

choices.label: D
choices.text: the trait passes only from fathers to all of their sons

hints.prompt: Mitochondria (and their DNA) are inherited almost entirely from which parent, via the egg?
hints.rationale: Mitochondria are transmitted maternally through the egg cytoplasm.

hints.prompt: Given maternal-only transmission of mitochondria, which observation would be INCONSISTENT with mitochondrial inheritance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: an affected father passing the trait to his sons

choices.label: B
choices.text: an affected mother passing it to her children

choices.label: C
choices.text: affected children born to affected mothers

hints.rationale: Fathers do not transmit mitochondria, so paternal transmission is inconsistent with the pattern.

hints.prompt: A tempting choice describes a trait where affected individuals usually have an affected parent and both sexes are affected equally. What inheritance is that?
hints.rationale: An affected parent with equal-sex transmission is the autosomal dominant pattern, a trap distinct from maternal mitochondrial inheritance.

hints.prompt: In Hardy-Weinberg, p and q are allele frequencies. Squaring a single allele's frequency gives the frequency of which kind of genotype?

hints.prompt: Some culture media serve special purposes beyond basic growth, such as favoring certain microbes or revealing their traits. In general, such special-purpose media are set apart from ordinary media by what?

choices.label: A
choices.text: an added feature designed for a specific goal

hints.rationale: Special-purpose media carry an added feature aimed at a specific goal.

hints.prompt: The stem asks about a type of MEDIUM. Which options are actually MICROBES (a salt-lover; an acid-lover) rather than media, and can be set aside?
hints.rationale: A halophile and an acidophile are microbes, not media, so they can be eliminated.

hints.prompt: A tempting distractor is 'a medium that favors some microbes while inhibiting others.' What is that?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a selective medium, which controls WHICH microbes grow, rather than revealing visible differences

choices.label: B
choices.text: a medium that reveals visible differences

hints.rationale: Favoring or inhibiting growth describes a selective medium, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: Which term best matches this description? isolation because species breed at different times
questions.explanation: The description matches temporal isolation.

hints.prompt: Two species in the same area may never interbreed if they are never reproductively active at the SAME TIME. Isolation based on breeding TIMING depends on what?
hints.rationale: Timing-based isolation depends on when each species breeds.

hints.prompt: Two options are a post-fertilization barrier and a physical-fit-mismatch barrier, not timing-based. Which two can be set aside?

choices.label: C
choices.text: the general-category (prezygotic barrier) and mechanical options

hints.rationale: A postzygotic barrier acts after fertilization and mechanical isolation is a physical mismatch, not timing-based, so those can be eliminated.

hints.prompt: A tempting distractor is the general term 'a prezygotic barrier.' Why is it not the best answer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is the broad CATEGORY; the stem asks for the specific timing-based type within it

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is more specific than timing-based isolation

hints.rationale: 'Prezygotic barrier' is the general category, broader than the specific timing-based type asked for, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: In immunology, what is the cell-mediated immune response?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a cytokine that carries signals between white blood cells

choices.label: B
choices.text: a molecule that coats microbes to promote their phagocytosis

choices.label: C
choices.text: the branch of adaptive immunity mediated by T cells

choices.label: D
choices.text: the branch of adaptive immunity mediated by antibodies

questions.explanation: the cell-mediated immune response is the branch of adaptive immunity mediated by T cells.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

hints.prompt: Adaptive immunity is split into two arms based on the main players. Those two arms are broadly based on what?

choices.label: A
choices.text: one arm using antibodies in fluids and one arm using T cells acting on cells

choices.label: B
choices.text: one arm using bones and one using blood

choices.label: C
choices.text: one arm for digestion and one for clotting

hints.rationale: The two adaptive arms are the antibody-based and the T-cell-based responses.

hints.prompt: The stem asks which option is the cell-mediated immune response (a whole branch). Which options are single MOLECULES (a signaling cytokine; a phagocytosis-promoting coat) rather than a branch, and can be set aside?
hints.rationale: A cytokine and an opsonin are molecules, not a branch of immunity, so they can be eliminated.

hints.prompt: A tempting distractor is 'the branch of adaptive immunity mediated by antibodies.' How does it differ?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is the antibody-based (humoral) branch, not the T-cell branch

hints.rationale: The antibody-based branch is humoral, distinct from the T-cell branch, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: Which vitamin is the precursor of thiamine pyrophosphate (TPP)?
questions.explanation: thiamine pyrophosphate (TPP) is derived from thiamine (vitamin B1).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

hints.prompt: B vitamins commonly serve as precursors to coenzymes that assist enzymes. In general, how is such a coenzyme related to its parent vitamin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the coenzyme is a chemically modified form of the vitamin

choices.label: B
choices.text: the vitamin is manufactured from the coenzyme

choices.label: C
choices.text: the vitamin and its coenzyme are unrelated

choices.label: D
choices.text: the coenzyme is a strand of DNA

hints.rationale: A vitamin-derived coenzyme is a chemically modified form of that vitamin.

hints.prompt: Two of the candidate vitamins form coenzymes for clearly different jobs: one for redox reactions (yielding NAD+/NADP+) and one for one-carbon transfer in nucleotide synthesis (yielding tetrahydrofolate). Which two can be set aside?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the niacin (redox) and folate (one-carbon transfer) options

hints.rationale: Niacin yields NAD+ (redox) and folate yields tetrahydrofolate (one-carbon transfer), roles unrelated to the stem's coenzyme, so those can be eliminated.

hints.prompt: A remaining distractor is pyridoxine (vitamin B6). What coenzyme does it form, and is it the cofactor named in the stem?

choices.label: A
choices.text: pyridoxal phosphate (PLP), used in amino-acid metabolism, not the stem's cofactor

choices.label: B
choices.text: it forms the stem's cofactor directly

choices.label: C
choices.text: it forms no coenzyme at all

choices.label: D
choices.text: it is chemically identical to niacin

hints.rationale: Pyridoxine yields PLP for amino-acid metabolism, a different cofactor, so it is a trap.
